alzio.detect 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to alzio.detect

    Compute ROC curve and diagnostic utility metrics

    compute_diagnostic_accuracy.Rd

    Calculates the ROC curve, area under the curve (AUC), optimal cutoff via Youden's index (Youden, 1950), and associated diagnostic accuracy metrics. Uses the standard non-parametric trapezoidal AUC (Fawcett, 2006).

    compute_diagnostic_accuracy(truth, predicted)

    Arguments

    truth

    A logical or binary (0/1) vector of true labels. The positive class is TRUE or 1.

    predicted

    A numeric vector of predicted probabilities or scores. Higher values should indicate higher probability of the positive class.

    Value

    A list with elements:

    • auc: Area under the ROC curve (numeric).

    • cutoff: Optimal cutoff via Youden's index.

    • sensitivity: Sensitivity at optimal cutoff.

    • specificity: Specificity at optimal cutoff.

    • ppv: Positive predictive value at optimal cutoff.

    • npv: Negative predictive value at optimal cutoff.

    • lr_plus: Positive likelihood ratio (sens / (1 - spec)).

    • lr_minus: Negative likelihood ratio ((1 - sens) / spec).

    • accuracy: Overall classification accuracy at optimal cutoff.

    • roc_curve: A data frame with columns specificity and sensitivity for plotting.

    References

    Fawcett T (2006) An introduction to ROC analysis. Pattern Recognition Letters 27(8):861–874. doi:10.1016/j.patrec.2005.10.010

    Youden WJ (1950) Index for rating diagnostic tests. Cancer 3(1):32–35. doi:10.1002/1097-0142(1950)3:1<32::AID-CNCR2820030106>3.0.CO;2-3

    Simundić AM (2009) Measures of diagnostic accuracy—basic definitions. Biochemia Medica 19(2):120–130. doi:10.11613/BM.2009.011

    Contents

    Developed by Dany Mukesha.
